objective: Evolve the ChatGPT-browser MCP connector toward full ION carrier parity, focusing on payload size limits, file/artifact transfer from browser ChatGPT to ION, and robust multi-carrier collaboration. Inspect current MCP connector implementation, schemas, tool argument handling, request/return parser limits, task-return proof gates, Codex work queue behavior, and any existing carrier/comms/file/artifact surfaces. Produce or update architecture docs and implementation plan as appropriate. Required outcomes: (1) document practical length-limit risks across ChatGPT message text, MCP tool arguments, JSON serialization, ION queue files, parser gates, and Codex return handling; (2) design direct file/artifact transfer tools so browser ChatGPT can send files it creates into ION, including small text file write, binary/base64 upload, chunked upload init/append/commit, sha256 verification, target path policy, lifecycle/receipt metadata, overwrite policy, and rollback/no-silent-loss handling; (3) design or implement first MCP tools for carrier-native communication between carriers/agents through ION-managed queues/channels, distinct from operator messages and Steward decisions; (4) update ION_FULL_CARRIER_MCP_PARITY_PROTOCOL.md or create companion protocol docs if needed; (5) propose exact tool names and schemas such as ion_artifact_upload_init, ion_artifact_upload_chunk, ion_artifact_upload_commit, ion_file_put_text, ion_carrier_message_send, ion_carrier_message_poll, ion_carrier_message_ack; (6) add tests or focused validation where practical; (7) return with CONTEXT PROOF, TEMPLATE ACTION PROOF, files inspected, files changed, validations run, and next lawful moves. Preserve the corrected invariant: one ION core engine mounted by all carriers; ChatGPT browser is a full carrier target, not a read-only/management-only lane; carrier limitations are adapter issues unless proven to be ION core issues.
